classification.domain: dietary intervention for functional GI disorders

topic.what: Meta-analysis confirming that a low-FODMAP diet significantly improves IBS symptoms including bloating and abdominal pain; findings extend to functional dyspepsia and gastritis contexts.
topic.how: Systematic review and meta-analysis pooling randomized controlled trials comparing low-FODMAP dietary intervention to control diets (standard dietary advice, high-FODMAP diet) in IBS and functional dyspepsia patients.
topic.why_matters: Provides the strongest available dietary evidence for fermentable carbohydrate restriction as a therapeutic tool in functional GI disorders with overlapping gastritis symptoms, supporting its application in functional dyspepsia management.

characteristics.what_it_measures: Degree of IBS symptom reduction (bloating, abdominal pain, stool consistency) with low-FODMAP vs. control diet
characteristics.value: significant IBS symptom reduction vs control
characteristics.vs_baseline: Standard dietary advice or high-FODMAP control diet
characteristics.evidence: Meta-analysis of multiple RCTs; overall symptom scores significantly lower in low-FODMAP groups
characteristics.context: Effect sizes vary across studies; strongest evidence for bloating and global symptom scores

characteristics.value: Moderate to high quality RCTs included; GRADE assessment supports moderate-to-strong recommendation
characteristics.evidence: Systematic review methodology with risk-of-bias assessment
characteristics.context: Dietary blinding is inherently difficult; some performance bias expected across trials

good_for: Justifying low-FODMAP dietary trials in functional dyspepsia and gastritis patients with bloating or pain
Clinical guideline development for dietary management of functional GI disorders
Designing dietary intervention arms in gastritis research
not_for: H. pylori-positive or erosive gastritis where dietary modification alone is insufficient
Long-term dietary prescriptions without dietitian supervision (FODMAP restriction may reduce beneficial prebiotic intake)
requires: Dietitian-led FODMAP restriction and reintroduction protocol for safe implementation
Differentiation of functional dyspepsia from organic gastritis before applying protocol

novelty: First meta-analytic synthesis confirming low-FODMAP efficacy across multiple RCTs in functional GI disorders
Establishes fermentable carbohydrate content (not just fiber type) as the operative dietary variable for symptom control
open_problems: Long-term effects of sustained FODMAP restriction on microbiome diversity and overall gut health require study
Optimal FODMAP thresholds for individual patients have not been established
Whether low-FODMAP benefits extend to organic (non-functional) gastritis populations is unexamined
